title: racinaea kalliantha
intro: Racinaea kalliantha, also known as the Andean air plant, is a species of epiphytic bromeliad that belongs to the family Bromeliaceae. This plant is traditionally used in the Andean region for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help to soothe wounds and fight off infections. The health benefits of Racinaea kalliantha include its ability to improve digestion, boost the immune system, and reduce fever, making it a valuable asset in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Racinaea kalliantha are typically made by infusing the plant's leaves in hot water to create a tea that can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
